title: clean horizontal strata — a hierarchy laid as legible layers, subject given weight
what_it_does_right: A bottom-up architecture is placed as even, full-width horizontal bands — one tier per row — so the ascent reads at a glance from the placement alone. The titular subject (the gateway seam) carries the visual weight as the focal band rather than being buried, peripheral tiers recede, and the return loop runs cleanly down the margin. The arrangement itself transmits the structure before a single label is read.
evidence: four even full-width strata; the subject tier given the most weight/glow; one clean bottom-to-top flow; the return routed at the frame edge, not across the interior; balanced spacing.
source: bdo, 2026-06-21 — looked at panel 3 (the tiers): 'this is nice'.
carries_intent: the placement reflects the topology so the hierarchy and its ascent are grasped at a glance
